name: Course Syllabus (Raw - Untrimmed)
content:
FULL ORIGINAL SYLLABUS TEXT INCLUDING:

Course: Advanced Algorithms (COT 4210)
Instructor: Dr. Topsakal
Office: ENB 118
Email: topsakal@usf.edu
Office Hours: MW 2-4pm

COURSE DESCRIPTION:
This course provides an in-depth study of algorithm design and analysis techniques. Students will learn to design efficient algorithms, analyze their complexity, and apply them to solve computational problems.

LEARNING OUTCOMES:
- Design and analyze efficient algorithms using divide and conquer, dynamic programming, and greedy approaches
- Evaluate algorithm efficiency using Big-O, Big-Theta, and Big-Omega notation
- Implement advanced data structures to optimize algorithm performance
- Apply graph algorithms to solve real-world problems
- Prove algorithm correctness using mathematical induction

TOPICS/WEEKLY SCHEDULE:
Week 1: Algorithm Analysis and Asymptotic Notation
Week 2: Divide and Conquer - Merge Sort, Quick Sort
Week 3: Dynamic Programming - Memoization and Tabulation
Week 4: Greedy Algorithms - Activity Selection, Huffman Coding
Week 5: Graph Algorithms - BFS, DFS, Shortest Paths
Week 6: Advanced Topics - NP-Completeness
Week 7: Midterm Exam
Week 8-14: Advanced topics and projects
Week 15: Final Exam

REQUIRED TEXTBOOK:
Introduction to Algorithms, 4th Edition by Cormen, Leiserson, Rivest, and Stein

UNIVERSITY POLICIES:

ACADEMIC INTEGRITY:
Students must adhere to USF's academic integrity policy. Any form of cheating, plagiarism, or unauthorized collaboration will result in disciplinary action ranging from a zero on the assignment to expulsion from the university.

ATTENDANCE POLICY:
Regular attendance is required and will be tracked. Missing more than 3 classes without valid documentation will result in a letter grade reduction.

GRADING SCALE:
- A: 90-100%
- B: 80-89%
- C: 70-79%
- D: 60-69%
- F: Below 60%

GRADING BREAKDOWN:
- Homework Assignments: 30%
- Midterm Exam: 25%
- Final Exam: 30%
- Projects: 15%

LATE WORK POLICY:
Late submissions receive a 10% penalty per day, up to 3 days. After 3 days, assignments will not be accepted.

DISABILITY ACCOMMODATIONS:
Students requiring accommodations should contact Students with Disabilities Services (SDS) at the beginning of the semester. Documentation must be provided to the instructor.

RELIGIOUS OBSERVANCES:
Students who anticipate the necessity of being absent from class due to religious observances should notify the instructor within the first two weeks of class.

CAMPUS FREE EXPRESSION:
USF supports the free exchange of ideas. Students are expected to engage in respectful dialogue.

EMERGENCY PROCEDURES:
In case of emergency, follow university protocols for evacuation or shelter-in-place.
